zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Sayer於1508年出生在英國艾可可爾斯特聖 波托爾福也斯 邱奇時，他的父親John Daniel Sayer26歲，母親Sarah Sayer Sayre Sears22歲。他於大約1526年與LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT在英國艾可可爾斯特可爾斯特 卡素結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1540年在荷蘭努-拉藍德阿姆斯特爾達姆享年32歲，埋葬在荷蘭努-拉藍德阿姆斯特爾達姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Sayer于1508年出生在英国艾可可爾斯特聖 波托爾福也斯 邱奇时，他的父亲John Daniel Sayer26岁，母亲Sarah Sayer Sayre Sears22岁。他于大约1526年与LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT在英国艾可可爾斯特可爾斯特 卡素结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1540年在荷兰努-拉藍德阿姆斯特爾達姆享年32岁，埋葬在荷兰努-拉藍德阿姆斯特爾達姆。

nameMeanings.name: Sayer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: common occupational name from Middle English sayer, a phonetic variant of Sawyer , which in Wales and neighboring English counties may have been reinforced by the synonymous Welsh saer ‘carpenter’ and everywhere by a possible borrowing into Middle English of Old French seieor, saieur ‘sawyer’.

English: rare occupational name perhaps denoting an assayer of metals, or a servant who tasted his lord's food before serving, from Middle English saiour ‘assayer, tester’ (a shortened form of Old French assaiour, ultimately from Late Latin exagium, a derivative of exagmināre ‘to weigh’).
English: rare occupational name for a professional reciter or minstrel, from Middle English seier(e) ‘speaker’.

variants.name: Sayre
meanings: English (Middlesex and Surrey): variant of   Sayer  .

variants.name: Sears
meanings: Irish (Kerry): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Saoghair ‘son of Saoghar’, a borrowing in Anglo-Norman Ireland of the Anglo-Norman French and Middle English personal name Saher, Seier (Old French Seheri), from ancient Germanic Sigeheri, Sighari, composed of the elements sig‘victory’ + hari, heri ‘army’. See Sayer 5.
English: variant of   Sayers  .
Americanized form of French (or French Canadian) Cyr and French Saint-Cyr (see St. Cyr ), with the addition of excrescent -s, a common feature of Americanized surnames.

variants.name: Sear
meanings: English: variant of   Sayer  .
Americanized form of French (or French Canadian)   Cyr  .

variants.name: Searson
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Saher, Seier (see Sayer 5) + -son.

English: variant of Searston, a habitational name from Syerston (Nottinghamshire). The placename probably derives from the Old English personal name Sighere (genitive Sigheres) + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.
Irish (Tipperary): variant of   Sears  .

variants.name: Sare
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Sayer  .
English: variant of   Sara  .
Croatian (Šare): nickname derived from šar(en) ‘mottled, gaily colored’, possibly applied to someone with hair of different colors. Compare Share .

variants.name: Saier
meanings: South German: occupational name for a farmer, from an agent derivative of Middle High German saien ‘to sow’. Compare Sayer 8.

variants.name: Say
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

variants.name: Sayers
meanings: English: variant of Sayer , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. This name is also established in Ireland.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Saoghair; see Sears .

variants.name: Sawyer
meanings: English: occupational name for someone who earned his living by sawing wood, from Middle English sauer(e), sauw(i)er, also sagh(i)er, sag(i)er ‘sawyer’, a derivative of Old English sagu ‘saw’.

Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname, or translation into English of Jewish Seger or some other surname meaning ‘sawyer’, e.g. German Sager and Slovenian Žagar (see Zagar ).

variants.name: Seekings
meanings: English: from the unrecorded Middle English personal name Saykin, a pet form of Middle English and Anglo-Norman French Saher, Seier (see Sayer 5). The final -s is post-medieval and excrescent.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.description: The Catholic King Charles I of Spain ruled the Netherlands.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Sayer was born in 1508, in St Botolph's Church, Colchester, Essex, England, his father, John Daniel Sayer, was 26 and his mother, Sarah Sayer Sayre Sears, was 22. He married LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT about 1526, in Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1540, in Amsterdam, North Holland, Netherlands, at the age of 32, and was buried in Amsterdam, North Holland, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Sayer est né en 1508, à St Botolph's Church, Colchester, Essex, Angleterre, son père, John Daniel Sayer, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Sarah Sayer Sayre Sears, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT environ 1526, à Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1540, à Amsterdam, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 32 ans, et a été enterré à Amsterdam, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas.

de.St Botolph's Church, Colchester, Essex,: St Botolph's Church, Colchester, Essex, England
de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Sayer 1508 in St Botolph's Church, Colchester, Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Daniel Sayer 26 und seine Mutter Sarah Sayer Sayre Sears 22. Er hat um 1526 in Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, England LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1540 im Alter von 32 Jahren in Amsterdam, Nord-Holland, Niederlande und wurde in Amsterdam, Nord-Holland, Niederlande begraben.

Lui sposato LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT circa 1526, nel Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1540, nel Amsterdam, Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 32 anni, e fu sepolto a Amsterdam, Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Sayerが1508年にイギリスエセックスコルチェスタースト ボトルフ'ス チャーチで生まれた時，父John Daniel Sayerは26歳，母Sarah Sayer Sayre Searsは22歳であった。約1526年にイギリスエセックスコルチェスターコルチェスター カストルでLADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETTと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1540年にオランダノオルド-hオランドアムステルダムで32歳で死去し，オランダノオルド-hオランドアムステルダムにて埋葬された。

ko.St Botolph's Church, Colchester, Essex,: 영국 에세스 코체스터 스트 벗프'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Sayer(이)가 1508년 영국 에세스 코체스터 스트 벗프'스 c후르치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Daniel Sayer(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Sarah Sayer Sayre Sears(은)는 22세였다. 그는 대략 1526년 영국 에세스 코체스터 코체스터 카스틀에서 LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1540년 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 아스터다에서 32세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 아스터다에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Sayer nasceu em 1508, em St Botolph's Church, Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Daniel Sayer, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Sarah Sayer Sayre Sears, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT aproximadamente 1526, em Colchester Castle, Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1540, em Amsterdam, Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos, com 32 anos, e foi sepultado em Amsterdam, Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos.

ru.St Botolph's Church, Colchester, Essex,: ст ботолф'с чурч, колчестир, эссекс, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Sayer родился в 1508 г. в местности ст ботолф'с чурч, колчестир, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени John Daniel Sayer было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Sarah Sayer Sayre Sears, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени LADY ANNE BOURCHIER KNYVETT около 1526 г. в местности колчестир кастл, колчестир, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1540 г. в местности Амстердам, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды в возрасте 32 лет и был похоронен в местности Амстердам, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды.
